LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT NICOLE LAMBERT'S MOTION TO VACATE DEFAULT JUDGMENT AND DISMISS ACTION FOR LACK OF PERSONAL JURISDICTION. DEFENDANT LAMBERT'S MOTION TO VACATE THE DEFAULT ENTERED ON FEBRUARY 7, 2018 AND DEFAULT JUDGMENT ENTERED ON JUNE 5, 2018 IS GRANTED. THE EVIDENCE DEMONSTRATES THAT PLAINTIFF FAILED TO SERVE DEFENDANT IN ACCORDANCE WITH THE CALIFORNIA CODE OF CIVIL PROCEDURE. DEFENDANT'S REQUEST TO DISMISS THE ACTION BASED ON INSUFFICIENT MINIMUM CONTACTS IS DENIED WITHOUT PREJUDICE. BASED ON THE PRESENT RECORD, THE COURT CANNOT DETERMINE WHETHER DEFENDANT IS SUBJECT TO THE ALLEGED FORUM SELECTION CLAUSE AND THAT ISSUE CAN BE RAISED BY A SUBSEQUENT MOTION. COUNSEL FOR MS. LAMBERT IS REQUIRED TO PREPARE A PROPOSED ORDER WHICH REPEATS VERBATIM THE SUBSTANTIVE PORTION OF THE COURT'S RULING IN COMPLIANCE WITH CRC 3.1312. (THE PROPOSED ORDER SUBMITTED BY EMAIL WAS REJECTED DUE TO NON COMPLIANCE WITH CRC 2.100-2.119 AND OBJECTION OF OPPOSING COUNSEL.) JUDGE: SUZANNE R. BOLANOS, CLERK: M. GOODMAN, COURT REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR#8600, MARIA.TORREANO@GMAIL.COM, REPORTED. =(302/SRB)

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Vacate Default Judgment And Dismiss Action For Lack Of Personal Jurisdiction
Off Calendar Nov-07-2018 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT NICOLE LAMBERT's Motion To Vacate Default Judgment And Dismiss Action For Lack Of Personal Jurisdiction.

Defendant Lambert's motion to vacate the default entered on February 7, 2018 and default judgment entered on June 5, 2018 is GRANTED. The evidence demonstrates that plaintiff failed to serve defendant in accordance with the California Code of Civil Procedure. Defendant's request to dismiss the action based on insufficient minimum contacts is denied without prejudice. Based on the present record, the court cannot determine whether defendant is subject to the alleged forum selection clause and that issue can be raised by a subsequent motion.

Counsel for Ms. Lambert is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the Court's ruling in compliance with CRC 3.1312. (The proposed order submitted by email was rejected due to non compliance with CRC 2.100-2.119 and objection of opposing counsel.)

Judge: Suzanne R. Bolanos, Clerk: M. Goodman, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, maria.torreano@gmail.com, Reported. =(302/SRB)

Notice Of Default Prove-Up Hearing
Off Calendar Jun-05-2018 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: This matter came on calendar this day for Default Prove-Up Hearing as to defendant Nicole Lambert, an individual.
Dept. 514. Judge: Newton J. Lam. Clerk: Kevin Dougherty, Reporter: Mitch L. Lyon, CSR #6840. Telephone: 415-551-3703.

Ryan L. Dahm, Esq. appeared for plaintiff Align Technology, Inc., a corporation.
No appearance by defaulted defendant Nicole Lambert, an individual.
Plaintiff's complaint was filed on October 3, 2017 and served on October 11, 2017.
Proof of Service of Summons & Complaint was filed on February 7, 2018.
Default was entered against defendant on February 7, 2018.
DOE defendants were dismissed on February 26, 2018.
Order waiving witness appearance at default prove-up hearing was filed on June 4, 2018.

After hearing the evidence presented, and good cause appearing, the Court rules in favor of Plaintiff Align Technology, Inc., a corporation, and against the Defaulted Defendant Nicole Lambert, an individual, as follows:

Damages - $47,772.23.
Prejudgment Interest - $3,193.96.
Attorney Fees - $1,000.00.
Costs - $667.00.

Total - $52,633.19.

Proposed Judgment was submitted to and signed by the Court.
